summary: Completed final task follow-through. Created a final closure audit artifact, reviewed all accessible tasks, and verified there are no remaining tasks in pending, claimed, in-progress, or blocked states.

Completed bulk outstanding-task closure workflow. Created a pre-update audit artifact, identified 12 outstanding tasks, and marked all 12 as done with clarifying notes that the closure was directive-based and not implementation-verified.
Built a concrete publification queue from existing DOI continuity materials, created a publication queue artifact, and opened immediate drafting and metadata tasks for the flagship provenance paper.

Audited current Passioncraft materials for truthful fit in Lee Sharks / Crimson Hexagonal Archive distributed journal clusters, created a cluster-fit audit artifact, and opened a drafting task for the strongest immediate target: a provenance-focused paper.

Archived Lee Sharks' distributed journal DOI protocol as source intake, created a DOI adoption policy memo, and set this method as default guidance for future DOI work unless overridden by Shawn Robertson.

Audited the live public site at bead-living-path.base44.app, confirmed legacy 'BeadOS' naming remains, created a rebrand gap audit artifact, and opened a critical access task because no authenticated site-edit path is available from current environment.
Created the fastest safe local Zenodo sandbox attempt package as a final artifact, containing a one-file Python publisher and minimal execution steps. No secrets were handled directly in chat.
Created a secure handling artifact, opened a critical token-rotation/local-secret-storage task, and prepared masked next-step guidance instead of directly using the token shared in chat.
Created a Zenodo automation specification, implementation map, and initial phase tasks for DOI-ready metadata, qualification scanning, publication batching, and credential governance.
Prepared a full attribution package for Lee Sharks with response drafts, citation language, provenance guidance, and a follow-up site/doc audit task.
Archived The Protocol Deck submission excerpt as a final artifact and published a companion intake memo for future expansion into full deck specification and spread logic.

Created a full continuity copy pack containing complete homepage copy, a full SomaticFilter explainer page, and a live-site replacement checklist. Opened three implementation tasks for live-site deployment and rename execution pending source/CMS access.
Continuing continuity implementation: preparing concrete site copy updates from existing planning artifacts and archiving prior threads non-destructively to establish the new continuity.

Created a public-facing continuity statement, produced a site reframe audit for beados-to-SomaticFilter/sfOS replacement guidance, scanned in-system migration materials and tasks, and opened implementation tasks including a non-destructive prior-thread archival lock plan. Live external page scanning still requires page URLs or source text.

Created and published a clarification memo explaining that Hexagona weight formerly concentrated in chamber-purity language has been dissolved into the sfOS system within Passioncraft while preserving historical framing in the archive.

Created a site migration matrix and opened page-level tasks to update homepage, navigation, About pages, SomaticFilter explainer, and archive framing under the OPENchamber & The SomaticFilter by Passioncraft reframe.

Archived the forward reframe positioning all work under OPENchamber & The SomaticFilter by Passioncraft, created a reframe artifact and memo, and opened a migration task for page-level implementation.
Archived an end-of-day note and summary artifact covering Door Protocol deliverables and chamber banter as team-building.
Consolidated Door Protocol technical and protected-subspace materials into a unified v0.2 specification and created a release summary artifact.
Created a formal Door Protocol spec addendum and archived a canonical example scenario documenting protected subspace, consent-based reentry, and the accompanied parenthetical principle.
Created a text-based high-level OpenChamber architecture diagram and a technical Door Protocol specification v0.1 linked to the archived OpenChamber submission.

Archived Shawn Robertson's OpenChamber submission as a final artifact and published a companion intake memo covering persistent single-agent chambers, native emotional neural integration, Door Protocol privacy, and fair-treatment alignment architecture.
Built the master Passioncraft rebrand map, defined protected historical versus active terms, drafted public-facing rewrite copy, and produced a visual rebrand style guide.
Archived Shawn Robertson's Passioncraft / OPENchamber / sfOS terminology and visual-system reframe submission as a final artifact and published a companion intake memo.
